testcase.name: [NONE][CKV_AWS_158] Ensure that CloudWatch Log Group is encrypted by KMS

failure.message: Ensure that CloudWatch Log Group is encrypted by KMS

testcase.failure:
Resource: aws_cloudwatch_log_group.lambda_log_group File: /lambda.tf: 47-52 Guideline: https://docs.prismacloud.io/en/enterprise-edition/policy-reference/aws-policies/aws-general-policies/ensure-that-cloudwatch-log-group-is-encrypted-by-kms
47 | resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_group" "lambda_log_group" { 48 | name = "/aws/lambda/${aws_lambda_function.my_lambda.function_name}" 49 | retention_in_days = 14 50 | # Depends on the Lambda function to ensure proper naming 51 | depends_on = [aws_lambda_function.my_lambda] 52 | }

failure.message: Ensure CloudWatch log groups retains logs for at least 1 year

testcase.failure:
Resource: aws_cloudwatch_log_group.lambda_log_group File: /lambda.tf: 47-52 Guideline: https://docs.prismacloud.io/en/enterprise-edition/policy-reference/aws-policies/aws-logging-policies/bc-aws-338
47 | resource "aws_cloudwatch_log_group" "lambda_log_group" { 48 | name = "/aws/lambda/${aws_lambda_function.my_lambda.function_name}" 49 | retention_in_days = 14 50 | # Depends on the Lambda function to ensure proper naming 51 | depends_on = [aws_lambda_function.my_lambda] 52 | }

testcase.name: [NONE][CKV_AWS_50] X-Ray tracing is enabled for Lambda

failure.message: X-Ray tracing is enabled for Lambda

testcase.failure:
Resource: aws_lambda_function.my_lambda File: /lambda.tf: 55-76 Guideline: https://docs.prismacloud.io/en/enterprise-edition/policy-reference/aws-policies/aws-serverless-policies/bc-aws-serverless-4
55 | resource "aws_lambda_function" "my_lambda" { 56 | function_name = "my-lambda-function" 57 | # Replace with your code package location 58 | filename = "lambda_function.zip" # Create this file or use S3 bucket 59 | source_code_hash = filebase64sha256("lambda_function.zip") 60 | 61 | # Replace with your handler and runtime as needed 62 | handler = "index.handler" 63 | runtime = "nodejs18.x" 64 | 65 | role = aws_iam_role.lambda_role.arn 66 | 67 | environment { 68 | variables = { 69 | ENVIRONMENT = "production" 70 | } 71 | } 72 | 73 | # Configure timeout and memory 74 | timeout = 30 75 | memory_size = 128 76 | }

testcase.name: [NONE][CKV_SECRET_2] AWS Access Key
testcase.failure:
Resource: 25910f981e85ca04baf359199dd0bd4a3ae738b6 File: /aws/dist/awscli/botocore/data/rds/2014-10-31/service-2.json: 16348-16349 Guideline: https://docs.prismacloud.io/en/enterprise-edition/policy-reference/secrets-policies/secrets-policy-index/git-secrets-2 16348 | "documentation":"

The Amazon Web Services KMS key identifier for encryption of the replicated automated backups. The KMS key ID is the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the KMS encryption key in the destination Amazon Web Services Region, for example, arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/AKIAI**********.

"

testcase.name: [NONE][CKV_SECRET_2] AWS Access Key
testcase.failure:
Resource: 25910f981e85ca04baf359199dd0bd4a3ae738b6 File: /aws/dist/awscli/botocore/data/sts/2011-06-15/service-2.json: 124-125 Guideline: https://docs.prismacloud.io/en/enterprise-edition/policy-reference/secrets-policies/secrets-policy-index/git-secrets-2 124 | "documentation":"

Returns the account identifier for the specified access key ID.

Access keys consist of two parts: an access key ID (for example, AKIAI**********) and a secret access key (for example, wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY). For more information about access keys, see Managing Access Keys for IAM Users in the IAM User Guide.

When you pass an access key ID to this operation, it returns the ID of the Amazon Web Services account to which the keys belong. Access key IDs beginning with AKIA are long-term credentials for an IAM user or the Amazon Web Services account root user. Access key IDs beginning with ASIA are temporary credentials that are created using STS operations. If the account in the response belongs to you, you can sign in as the root user and review your root user access keys. Then, you can pull a credentials report to learn which IAM user owns the keys. To learn who requested the temporary credentials for an ASIA access key, view the STS events in your CloudTrail logs in the IAM User Guide.

This operation does not indicate the state of the access key. The key might be active, inactive, or deleted. Active keys might not have permissions to perform an operation. Providing a deleted access key might return an error that the key doesn't exist.

"

testcase.name: [NONE][CKV_SECRET_6] Base64 High Entropy String
testcase.failure:
